acceptance_targets: [chapter_06_award_01] PASS if the revised aftermath at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:127 and the revised close at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:249 show Gavin's skewed priorities through observable behavior and at most one brief self-diagnostic line in each section; FAIL if either section still spends multiple consecutive paragraphs explaining to the reader that he knows his response is morally wrong.

[chapter_06_craft_01] PASS if a reader can trace the manuscript from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:19 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:245 without any unexplained relocation; FAIL if Gavin reaches for the trunk at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:135 while the object is still later treated as being under the pillow without a described move.

[chapter_06_dialogue_01] PASS if the revised speech at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:185 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:186 explains the substrate without the sentence-and-grammar calibration phrasing and includes at least one Gavin-style self-correction or hedge; FAIL if a reader can still match the line directly to the style-bible example by wording or structure.

[full_award_002] Pass if the revised speech at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1196 and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1200 still shows Gavin's halting self-correction and inadequacy under pressure, but no longer reproduces the calibration line's spell/sentence/grammar sequence or its closing beat. Fail if a reader can still hear the sample line hiding inside the revision.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised aftermath at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:127 and the revised close at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:249 show Gavin's skewed priorities through observable behavior and at most one brief self-diagnostic line in each section; FAIL if either section still spends multiple consecutive paragraphs explaining to the reader that he knows his response is morally wrong.
findings.problem: The chapter does not trust its own high-wire premise: instead of letting Gavin's deadpan irritation at lost translation time collide with the shared torture around him, the narration repeatedly explains that his response is wrong and rehearses both sides of the moral argument. That explanatory framing blunts the dark-comic discomfort the chapter spine says should land deadpan.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the aftermath and close around concrete choices at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:127, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:129, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:135, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:249, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:251. Keep Gavin's wrong priorities visible through what he notices, what he almost does, and what he fails to do for Duncan or Miren; cut the abstract adjudication unless a line introduces new pressure.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if a reader can trace the manuscript from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:19 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:245 without any unexplained relocation; FAIL if Gavin reaches for the trunk at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:135 while the object is still later treated as being under the pillow without a described move.
findings.problem: The manuscript's location contradicts itself inside the chapter. Gavin hides it under his pillow before the summons, later thinks he needs to go to his trunk to retrieve it, then the text reverts to the manuscript being under his pillow. That unsupported object jump breaks local continuity in a chapter built around the manuscript's pull.
findings.rewrite_direction: Make the manuscript's hiding place continuous across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:19, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:135, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:231, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:245. Either keep it under the pillow all night or add an on-page action where Gavin moves it to the trunk before the common-room aftermath.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised speech at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:185 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:186 explains the substrate without the sentence-and-grammar calibration phrasing and includes at least one Gavin-style self-correction or hedge; FAIL if a reader can still match the line directly to the style-bible example by wording or structure.
findings.problem: Gavin's explanation lifts a style-bible calibration line almost verbatim: the 'spell is a sentence / grammar / grammar's wrong too' phrasing is too close to the forbidden example text. Calibration lines are not reusable dialogue, and this one reads as template reuse rather than fresh speech.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace Gavin's formulation at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:185 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:186 with new language that still sounds like him: apologetic, self-correcting, ordinary, and struggling for analogy. Keep the failed explanation dynamic, but do not reuse the sentence-and-grammar scaffold or the phrase 'Except grammar's wrong too.'

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised speech at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1196 and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1200 still shows Gavin's halting self-correction and inadequacy under pressure, but no longer reproduces the calibration line's spell/sentence/grammar sequence or its closing beat. Fail if a reader can still hear the sample line hiding inside the revision.
findings.problem: Gavin's explanation to Miren lifts a style-bible example line into the chapter almost intact: the spell/sentence/grammar ladder and the 'grammar's wrong too' beat are calibration material, not finished prose. Because the manuscript uses the sample line so directly, the scene sounds guided by the bible instead of spoken under pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace Gavin's explanation centered on snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1196 and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1200 with a freshly composed failure-to-explain. Keep the same dramatic function: he tries, self-corrects, reaches for ordinary language, and still cannot make Miren understand. But avoid the exact sentence/grammar architecture from the style bible; find a different everyday frame of reference or let him abort the analogy sooner so the line feels stumbled into rather than imported.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's central risk is tonal: it must sustain dark comedy, cosmic horror, and genuine emotional tenderness in the same narrative without any register canceling the others. Lean into the discomfort of juxtaposition. A scene where Gavin is mentally modeling ward-substrate decay while a classmate screams under the Cruciatus Curse is not a failure of empathy — it is the novel's core tension made visceral. The humor of the premise — that Voldemort's war is an irritating distraction from the real problem — must never be defused by making Gavin feel appropriately guilty at the right moments. He should feel guilty sometimes and not other times, and the reader should sit with the discomfort of a protagonist whose priorities have become genuinely alien. The cosmic material should be rendered with enough specificity that it is genuinely unsettling, not just philosophically interesting. Do not soften the ending: Gavin's refusal to intervene in the Battle of Hogwarts must be played straight, without a last-minute redemptive gesture or moral safety net.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in this novel is a particularly acute failure because the entire thematic engine runs on the difference between seeing something directly and not seeing it. Gavin's journey is toward unmediated perception. The prose must mirror that commitment. When the Carrows torture a student, render the torture. When Gavin is hit with the Cruciatus, render the pain in his body. When the cosmic threat presses against perception, render what it feels like — do not retreat to abstractions about 'something vast' or 'an incomprehensible presence.' Find the specific sensory detail that makes the incomprehensible land. Euphemism in a novel about the failure to see what is real is thematic self-sabotage.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all dialogue. Uncontracted forms are reserved for deliberate emphasis, Babbling's academic register, or moments where a character is being pointedly formal. If a line reads stiffly, check contractions first.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter textures aggressively. A high-intensity chapter (Cruciatus detention, Duncan's torture) must be followed by a chapter with a different pressure profile — quieter, more internal, more comic, or more focused on intellectual work. No two consecutive chapters should share the same dominant emotional register, the same scene-structure (e.g., two chapters that each build to a confrontation), or the same pacing profile. Vary scene count, dialogue density, and the ratio of interiority to action. Some chapters should be mostly one extended scene; others should move across multiple locations and timeframes. The Christmas chapter and the work-routine chapters should breathe differently from the Carrow chapters and the battle chapter.
prose_style_profile.diction: Plain, concrete, specific. Anglo-Saxon roots preferred over Latinate where both are available. Avoid poetic diction, literary allusion, and metaphors that call attention to their own cleverness. Metaphors should arise from Gavin's actual frame of reference: schoolwork, the castle, ordinary physical experience. When the First Art requires description, reach for precision over beauty — describe what it looks like, not what it means. The novel's most striking language should come from the collision between ordinary diction and extraordinary perception.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, locked to Gavin's perception throughout. The narrative voice has access to his thoughts, feelings, and sensory experience but not to anyone else's interiority. Other characters are known only through what Gavin observes. As the First Art restructures Gavin's cognition, the narrative's perceptual range should gradually expand — he begins noticing things no one else can see — but the voice remains grounded in his ordinary, slightly bewildered perspective. The narration should never become more articulate or literary than Gavin himself would be; the strangeness of what he perceives should press against the ordinariness of how he describes it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and responsive to scene pressure. Default rhythm is medium-paced prose with sentences of mixed length — some short, some compound, none habitually long. During scenes of intellectual discovery, let sentences extend as Gavin's attention extends, then cut short when comprehension fails. During Carrow scenes, prose should tighten and become more physical. During quiet scenes, allow room for observation and sensory detail without rushing toward the next event. Do not develop a signature rhythm that persists across all chapters — the rhythm should be the scene's rhythm, not the author's.
